title: Boole Functions and Boole Algebra
summary: Introduces the algebraic structure of Boole algebra, defines Boole functions, truth tables, and the complete set of axioms and theorems including De Morgan laws.
content_points: Boole algebra: set B={0,1} with operations +, ·, ¯ satisfying identity, constants, idempotence, involution, complementarity, commutativity, associativity, distributivity.
A Boole function f: B^n → B^m; there are (2^m)^(2^n) distinct such functions.
Computers use 2-level digital signals; combinational circuits implement Boole functions.
Key theorems: unification, absorption, De Morgan (X+Y)¯ = X¯·Y¯ and (X·Y)¯ = X¯+Y¯, duality.
Truth tables enumerate all 2^n input combinations with corresponding outputs.

formulas.name: Number of n-input m-output Boole functions
De Morgan (1)
formulas.expression: NOT(X OR Y) = (NOT X) AND (NOT Y)

formulas.name: De Morgan (2)
formulas.expression: NOT(X AND Y) = (NOT X) OR (NOT Y)

learning_objectives: State the axioms and theorems of Boole algebra
Construct truth tables for given Boole functions
Apply De Morgan and other laws to simplify expressions
